body.title: agent_datacenter: spike Dovecot vs embedded Python IMAP, document decision
body.result: Spike complete: Python asyncio stub works at ~80 lines (APPEND+IDLE verified). Decision: Dovecot for production, Python stub for tests. docs/decisions/imap-impl.md written. Spike at lab/spikes/imap_stub_spike.py.
body.description:
The IMAP bus is core infrastructure. Evaluate Dovecot vs embedded Python before any IMAP code is written — the decision affects deployment, testing, and the portable-bootstrap story.

Problem: Two viable approaches with different tradeoffs. Must commit before writing T-adc-imap-server-embedded.

Proposed shape:
- Dovecot: system dep (apt install dovecot-imapd), text-file config, mature IDLE support, Expire plugin for 24hr retention, requires system privileges at install time
- Python embedded: options are imaplib-based (low-level) or pure-Python IMAP server libs; no system dep, controllable from inside the package, testable in-process, less mature
- Recommended approach: Dovecot for production deploys; thin Python stub for test fixtures (enough IMAP for tests to run without system Dovecot)
- Spike: attempt `import imaplib; start minimal Python IMAP server` — verify feasibility in 1hr; if not feasible, Dovecot is the only choice
- Write docs/decisions/imap-impl.md with: options, tradeoffs, chosen impl, test-stub plan

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/docs/decisions/imap-impl.md (new)

Design rules:
- no-sqlite: chosen implementation must not introduce SQLite (Dovecot uses its own storage; Python impl must not use sqlite3 for message storage)

Scope boundary:
IN: evaluation, spike attempt, decision doc
OUT: any production IMAP server code (that is T-adc-imap-server-embedded, Phase 1)

Test plan:
- docs/decisions/imap-impl.md exists with decision clearly stated
- If Python stub is viable: a 50-line proof-of-concept that accepts IMAP APPEND and IDLE exists in lab/spikes/
